title: Changing architecture uses previous architecture for base image
body:
**Description**

I'm trying to create a cross-arch image using a base image pulled from Docker (`debian`), but I made a mistake with the architecture the first time round. I changed the architecture used in `buildah from`, but the cached image (with the wrong architecture) is used.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `buildah from --arch arm64 debian`, confirm that the working container is arm64.
2. `buildah from --arch 386 debian`, the working container is still arm64 (and no image pull occurred).


**Describe the results you received:**

Incorrect architecture of subsequent working containers, in this example it is still arm64

**Describe the results you expected:**

Buildah should notice that the cached image has the wrong architecture and should fetch the right one.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/glinux-rodete-base-20210518.01.00,now 1.19.6+dfsg1-1 amd64 [installed,automatic]
buildah/glinux-rodete-base-20210518.01.00 1.19.6+dfsg1-1 i386
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.19.6
Go Version:      go1.15.8
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.10.3
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_CODENAME=rodete
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Debian GNU/Linux rodete"
DISTRIB_ID=Debian
DISTRIB_RELEASE=rodete
GOOGLE_CODENAME=rodete
GOOGLE_ID=Goobuntu
GOOGLE_RELEASE="rodete 20210518.01.00RL"
GOOGLE_ROLE=laptop
GOOGLE_TRACK=stable
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux rodete"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"
ID=debian
VERSION_CODENAME=rodete
VERSION_ID=rodete
HOME_URL="https://go/glinux"
SUPPORT_URL="https://go/techstop"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://go/techstop"
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux rmccorkell 5.10.28-1rodete1-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 5.10.28-1rodete1 (2021-04-30) x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
<no such file>
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

I can't reproduce with Buildah v1.21.0.  We had some bigger changes in the image code which seems to have fixed that issue.
Thanks for confirming! I'll wait for my distro to update to 1.12, in the meantime the workaround is to delete the existing cached image before running `buildah from` with a different arch.
